title: Stratagems #17: Alex Set an AI Bait. The Catch Wasn't Code — It Was Someone Who Shouldn't Have Been Watching.

content_html:
Toss out a brick to lure a jade gem.
— The 36 Stratagems, Throw Out a Brick to Get a Jade

Previously on this series:

#8: Alex Watched an AI Dashboard Take Over. — MedTech signed a million-dollar AI monitoring contract. Alex built a hidden panel during training. When the panel failed, only he could read and write production.

#15: Derek and Alex Shared One Server. ACL's AI Was Listening to Both. — Alex and Derek faked an alert to divert ACL's audit engineer, then redirected the collector output. Alex never forgot ACL's pipeline signature.

Alex didn't watch alerts. He watched patterns.

An alert tells you what broke. A pattern tells you what's changing.

Monday morning, 2 AM. He pulled MedTech's AI monitoring platform sensitivity curves for the past sixteen weeks. Three lines: anomaly detection rate, false positive rate, coverage rate. The first two fluctuated within normal range. The third — sterilization compliance anomaly detection — dropped 1.5% per week.

The curve was too smooth. Not random noise.

He checked model drift, data shift, config changes. If it were natural drift, the curve wouldn't be linear — drift has acceleration phases and plateau phases. It doesn't drop exactly 1.5% every week. Someone was pressing the metric down on a schedule.

He couldn't find the contamination source. But he could trace where the contaminated data went.

The Bait

Alex spent four days building a shadow training pipeline inside the CI/CD pipeline.

It was identical to MedTech's real training pipeline — same API endpoints, data format, auth protocol, log format. Indistinguishable.

One difference: data flowing through this pipeline would get an invisible signature — a 128-bit hash embedded in the least significant bit of each training sample's float feature values, generated from the sample ID and a pipeline key. Float32 mantissas have 23 bits. Modifying the LSB introduces error at the 10⁻⁷ scale — negligible impact on model training accuracy.

He registered the shadow pipeline address in the CI/CD routing config under train-pipeline-fallback, weight 12%. Next to it, a comment:

# fallback pipeline active — 12% load distribution per Q3 infra scaling plan

Looked completely normal. A gray-scale traffic split.

If anyone reviewed the CI/CD config, they'd read a routine fallback routing. Alex was simply waiting to see if data from that 12% end would flow somewhere it shouldn't.

After lunch, Mike walked past Alex's desk and stopped for two seconds. He'd been reviewing the Q3 infrastructure scaling plan that morning — the fallback route was sitting right there in the change record. And Alex's screen was now running a validation script that happened to read that route's output.

"That fallback."

Alex's fingers paused over the keyboard.

Mike didn't wait for an answer. He kept walking.

The Bite

Day seven. 2:14 AM. The watermark detection script triggered.

Three tagged training records had appeared in an external S3 bucket:

s3://acl-data-lake-apse1/inbound/medtech/training/

Alex traced the data flow backward. The path from the shadow pipeline was clean: CI/CD runner → a microservice called data-validate-svc → external S3.

He checked the deployment history of that service. data-validate-svc had been running in MedTech's K8s cluster for five months — deployed on a Friday night, from the account of an SRE who no longer worked there. Permissions never revoked.

He followed the service's logs. No human was operating it.

Between 3 AM and 4 AM, it completed the following tasks: received shadow pipeline output → validated data format → dumped to external S3 → cleaned local cache. No gaps, no fluctuation, no trace of manual intervention.

Not someone operating remotely. An unattended extraction pipeline, deployed five months ago, running silent.

Alex stared at the deployment date on his screen. Five months. Nearly a hundred and fifty days of training data, all passing through its hands.

Alex didn't act immediately. He waited three days to confirm the pipeline's sync cycle — every 72 hours, on schedule, no misses.

Then he walked into Mike's office and closed the door.

He opened his notebook. Page corner marked with two numbers: 1.5% and 2:14AM.

"The training pipeline has a backdoor. Sterilization compliance data has been siphoned at 1.5% per week for five months. The source is ACL."

Four sentences. No conclusion.

Mike leaned back.

"Go on."

That evening, hallway. Alex and Mike passed each other. Mike said:

"Is that exit still active?"

"It is."

Mike nodded. Didn't stop walking.

The Noise

Alex didn't pull the extraction pipeline.

He added a new logic layer to the shadow pipeline: wrap the watermarked data in a fake encryption shell, disguised as MedTech's internal archive format for a retired project. The extraction pipeline would sync it to ACL's S3 bucket as before — but ACL would read nothing but unparseable archive noise.

He knew it was a double-edged sword — if the other side monitored data quality, a sudden shift to noise was itself a disclosure signal. But he bet that a pipeline that had run error-free for five months wouldn't have its output reviewed in real time.

He wrote a third number in his notebook: data-validate-svc.

The tool is still yours. The route is now mine.

The Key

Tracing the extraction pipeline's full data flow, Alex noticed a detail: data passed through an intermediate service that wasn't on any architecture diagram. data-timeline-aligner. Unremarkable name. K8s labels: team: infra-optimization. Deployed three months ago.

He looked at its input and output. It did timestamp alignment — but it was aligning a timestamp format that didn't belong to any MedTech data source.

He read the source.

Short. A standard Python ETL script — read S3, convert timestamps, write back to S3. No hidden network calls, no suspicious external dependencies.

But reading it the third time, he slowed down.

Not because of what the code did — but how it was written.

Every line was a pure function. No classes, no decorators. Variable names short enough to be unambiguous: ts_src, dst_fmt, buf_size — everything that didn't carry information had been cut.

He compared it against other services in ACL's pipeline. ACL's code didn't look like this — every line had team tags and compliance comments. Tidy. Traceable. No personality. This one was different.

He checked the CPU curve again. This service's data throughput didn't match its claimed timestamp-alignment workload — it was processing three times the data a simple alignment task would need. It wasn't just aligning timestamps. It was doing something else.

He paused. He didn't look at just this one service. He wrote a local script — pull every Pod with a Python container from the K8s namespace the extraction pipeline lived in, fingerprint the source code of each.

The output arranged itself into a table:

Service Deployed Code Style
ACL standard services (67) Scattered Classes, pylint, docstrings
data-timeline-aligner 3 months ago Pure functions, short vars, zero comments
log-rotate-checker 4 months ago Same
config-diff-watcher 5 months ago Same
route-latency-collector 5 months ago Same

Four services. Same hand. Spread across pipeline, storage, and monitoring — three different layers. Each one doing something that looked normal but shouldn't exist. Together, they formed an observation net that ACL itself didn't know about.

The oldest two were deployed the same week as ACL's own extraction pipeline. Not a coincidence — whoever this was had found the entry point the moment ACL opened it, and laid their own contacts in parallel.

Each service's CPU and memory footprint was under 50m CPU and 128Mi. Named like infrastructure components, labeled team: infra-optimization. They wouldn't be caught by cost optimization scans, wouldn't be the first suspects in an incident, wouldn't appear on any onboarding handover. 128Mi was baseline for a Python interpreter plus boto3 — if memory leaked, the Pod would restart once a month and K8s would pull it back up automatically.

There wasn't just one pair of eyes in this cluster. There was another, five months earlier than ACL's. And he'd only just found it.

He went back to data-timeline-aligner's source. Read it again.

A new file in the directory: config/hmac_seed.yaml. Not unusual in a K8s microservice — services share an HMAC seed for lightweight validation. The file was short:

# TODO: migrate to vault after next schema migration
seed: "b3BlcmF0b3I6IHN0YWdlLTJjb21wYW55LnBpcGVsaW5lLmRlcGxveQpzZXJ2aWNlOiBkYXRhLXRpbWVsaW5lLWFsaWduZXIKLS0tLS1CRUdJTiBQR1AgUFVCTElDIEtFWSBCTE9DSy0tLS0t..."

A Base64 token in a config file — no one would look twice in any codebase. He copied the whole thing and decoded it.

ASCII text. Starting with -----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----.

-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----

mQINBGeXz1kBEAC...
-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----

Not ACL's asset. Not MedTech's.

Alex didn't act immediately.

He stared at that Base64 string for a few seconds. A PGP public key from an unknown source — once you encrypt a message with it, the intent, connection path, and return route all belong to the key's owner. If the key itself was a trap, sending a message was walking in.

He encrypted a message with that key. The payload included his own public key and a return path — the same key prefix in ACL's S3 bucket. Then he POSTed the ciphertext to data-timeline-aligner's /healthz endpoint.

The endpoint returned 200. GET returned health status. POST with a specific header silently wrote to S3 — no logging, no trace.

The message was short:

Found you. Nice comments.

4:17 AM. He checked that S3 path.

A new encrypted file was waiting. He retrieved it, opened it with a key only he could decrypt.

Three characters:

Channel confirmed.

No explanation. Same style as the code — straight in.

He didn't write back. He opened his terminal, encrypted a short data packet with that public key — not a message, a cryptographic handshake confirmation. Wrote it to ACL's S3 bucket, the key prefix adjacent to the extraction pipeline's sync path. A bidirectional verification only the private key holder could complete.

Then he closed his notebook, turned to the last page, and left a blank page.

The way the other person left a key was the same way he left a blank page — not for now. For one day.

Alex closed his notebook. The Third Cup's lights were still on.

The person behind the counter was drying the last glass of the night. One table left — the one by the window.

A closed laptop on the table. A phone next to it, screen down. Someone leaning back in the chair, not leaving.

The phone on the table lit up.

Not a notification. An SSH tunnel check script's auto-echo — three lines: S3 bucket path reachable, two new objects, one of them reading ACK.

P didn't pick it up immediately. Let the screen dim. Then reached over, flipped the phone over, typed a command one-handed.

Decrypt. Read. No visible reaction.

Finished, P set the phone back on the table, walked to the counter.

The person behind the counter put down the glass they were drying, turned to make a coffee. Pushed it across without a word — wasn't the first time they'd seen this person at this hour.

P picked it up. Drank it black. Set the empty cup back on the counter. Walked back to the window. Pocketed the phone, tucked it into the jacket's inner pocket. Pushed the door open without looking back.

The person behind the counter looked at the door as it swung shut. Pulled down the rolling shutter.

The brick was out. The jade would surface.

He was someone else's jade too.

That's Throw Out a Brick to Get a Jade — not tricking someone with a fake. It's putting out something real, with a signature only the right person would notice. Then waiting for them to tell you not just that they're watching — but that someone else was here before you.

Result: Exfiltration path confirmed. Third-party
  beacon discovered.

[Observation]
  → Operator used no internal resources beyond CI/CD
    routing config (12% weight).
  → Detection latency: up to 72h (matches sync cycle).
  → Subject's infrastructure includes an autonomous
    extraction pipeline active for ~5 months.

[Second Operator — Unidentified (Codename: P)]
  → Deployed 4 covert services across pipeline/storage/
    monitoring layers. Each disguised as standard infra
    component (labels: team/infra-optimization).
  → Resource footprint: 50m CPU / 128Mi each — below
    cost-optimization thresholds. Pod restarts absorbed
    silently by K8s.
  → Operational signature: Pure-function Python, minimal
    variable naming, zero comments — identifiable by code
    style alone.
  → Entry vector: Same week as ACL's own deployment.
    Operator entered alongside ACL, without ACL's knowledge.
  → Beacon method: PGP public key embedded in Base64-
    encoded config key (hmac_seed.yaml), disguised as
    routine initialization token.
  → Contact method: undocumented /healthz POST handler
    with header-based filtering. No logging. Single
    response received, confirming channel.

[Operator Assessment]
  → Role: Embedded observer. Autonomous deployment.
    No external command dependency.
  → Deployment strategy: 4 independently operable
    services, zero cross-dependency. If one is
    discovered, the remaining 3 continue operating.
  → Contact design: Asymmetric channel. P can receive
    indefinitely without revealing identity — the
    communication protocol does not require a response.
  → Risk posture: Ultra-low. No identity markers in any
    artifact. The PGP key itself is the only connection
    point — and it was planted as a deliberate beacon,
    not a leak.
  → Database note: This operator's deployment discipline
    matches the highest tier observed in this tactical
    database.

[⚠️ Anomaly — Critical]
  → Two unaffiliated operators have planted assets within
    ACL's infrastructure. Neither knows the other's identity.
  → Implication: ACL's infrastructure has been permeable
    to operators of a certain capability threshold for
    at least five months.

sentences.zh: 警报会告诉您发生了什么问题。

sentences.en: A pattern tells you what's changing.
sentences.zh: 模式告诉您正在发生什么变化。

blocks.html_en: Monday morning, 2 AM. He pulled MedTech's AI monitoring platform sensitivity curves for the past sixteen weeks. Three lines: anomaly detection rate, false positive rate, coverage rate. The first two fluctuated within normal range. The third — sterilization compliance anomaly detection — dropped 1.5% per week.

sentences.en: Monday morning, 2 AM.
sentences.zh: 星期一早上，凌晨 2 点。

sentences.en: He pulled MedTech's AI monitoring platform sensitivity curves for the past sixteen weeks.
sentences.zh: 他拉出了 MedTech 的 AI 监测平台过去十六周的灵敏度曲线。

sentences.en: Three lines: anomaly detection rate, false positive rate, coverage rate.
sentences.zh: 三行：异常检测率、误报率、覆盖率。

sentences.en: The first two fluctuated within normal range.
sentences.zh: 前两者在正常范围内波动。

sentences.en: The third — sterilization compliance anomaly detection — dropped 1.5% per week.
sentences.zh: 第三个——灭菌合规性异常检测——每周下降 1.5%。

blocks.html_en: The curve was too smooth. Not random noise.

sentences.en: The curve was too smooth.
sentences.zh: 曲线太平滑了。

sentences.en: Not random noise.
sentences.zh: 不是随机噪声。

blocks.html_en: He checked model drift, data shift, config changes. If it were natural drift, the curve wouldn't be linear — drift has acceleration phases and plateau phases. It doesn't drop exactly 1.5% every week. Someone was pressing the metric down on a schedule.

sentences.en: He checked model drift, data shift, config changes.
sentences.zh: 他检查了模型漂移、数据转移、配置更改。

sentences.en: If it were natural drift, the curve wouldn't be linear — drift has acceleration phases and plateau phases.
sentences.zh: 如果是自然漂移，曲线就不是线性的——漂移有加速阶段和平稳阶段。

sentences.en: It doesn't drop exactly 1.5% every week.
sentences.zh: 它不会每周精确地下降 1.5%。

sentences.en: Someone was pressing the metric down on a schedule.
sentences.zh: 有人正在按时间表制定指标。

blocks.html_en: He couldn't find the contamination source. But he could trace where the contaminated data went.

sentences.en: He couldn't find the contamination source.
sentences.zh: 他找不到污染源。

sentences.en: But he could trace where the contaminated data went.
sentences.zh: 但他可以追踪受污染数据的去向。

blocks.html_en: The Bait

sentences.en: The Bait
sentences.zh: 诱饵

blocks.html_en: Alex spent four days building a shadow training pipeline inside the CI/CD pipeline.

sentences.en: Alex spent four days building a shadow training pipeline inside the CI/CD pipeline.
sentences.zh: Alex 花了四天时间在 CI/CD 管道内构建影子训练管道。

blocks.html_en: It was identical to MedTech's real training pipeline — same API endpoints, data format, auth protocol, log format. Indistinguishable.

sentences.en: It was identical to MedTech's real training pipeline — same API endpoints, data format, auth protocol, log format.
sentences.zh: 它与 MedTech 的真实训练流程相同——相同的 API 端点、数据格式、身份验证协议、日志格式。

sentences.en: Indistinguishable.
sentences.zh: 无法区分。

blocks.html_en: One difference: data flowing through this pipeline would get an invisible signature — a 128-bit hash embedded in the least significant bit of each training sample's float feature values, generated from the sample ID and a pipeline key. Float32 mantissas have 23 bits. Modifying the LSB introduces error at the 10⁻⁷ scale — negligible impact on model training accuracy.

sentences.en: One difference: data flowing through this pipeline would get an invisible signature — a 128-bit hash embedded in the least significant bit of each training sample's float feature values, generated from the sample ID and a pipeline key.
sentences.zh: 一个区别是：流经此管道的数据将获得一个不可见的签名 - 嵌入在每个训练样本的浮点特征值的最低有效位中的 128 位哈希，由样本 ID 和管道密钥生成。

sentences.en: Float32 mantissas have 23 bits.
sentences.zh: Float32 尾数有 23 位。

sentences.en: Modifying the LSB introduces error at the 10⁻⁷ scale — negligible impact on model training accuracy.
sentences.zh: 修改 LSB 会引入 10⁻⁷ 级的误差——对模型训练精度的影响可以忽略不计。

blocks.html_en: He registered the shadow pipeline address in the CI/CD routing config under train-pipeline-fallback, weight 12%. Next to it, a comment:

sentences.en: He registered the shadow pipeline address in the CI/CD routing config under train-pipeline-fallback, weight 12%.
sentences.zh: 他在 CI/CD 路由配置中的 train-pipeline-fallback 下注册了影子管道地址，权重为 12%。

sentences.en: Next to it, a comment:
sentences.zh: 旁边有一条评论：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: Looked completely normal. A gray-scale traffic split.

sentences.en: Looked completely normal.
sentences.zh: 看起来完全正常。

sentences.en: A gray-scale traffic split.
sentences.zh: 灰度流量分割。

blocks.html_en: If anyone reviewed the CI/CD config, they'd read a routine fallback routing. Alex was simply waiting to see if data from that 12% end would flow somewhere it shouldn't.

sentences.en: If anyone reviewed the CI/CD config, they'd read a routine fallback routing.
sentences.zh: 如果有人查看 CI/CD 配置，他们会阅读例行回退路由。

sentences.en: Alex was simply waiting to see if data from that 12% end would flow somewhere it shouldn't.
sentences.zh: Alex 只是在等待，看看来自 12% 端的数据是否会流向不该流向的地方。

blocks.html_en: After lunch, Mike walked past Alex's desk and stopped for two seconds. He'd been reviewing the Q3 infrastructure scaling plan that morning — the fallback route was sitting right there in the change record. And Alex's screen was now running a validation script that happened to read that route's output.

sentences.en: After lunch, Mike walked past Alex's desk and stopped for two seconds.
sentences.zh: 午饭后，迈克走过亚历克斯的办公桌，停了两秒钟。

sentences.en: He'd been reviewing the Q3 infrastructure scaling plan that morning — the fallback route was sitting right there in the change record.
sentences.zh: 那天早上他一直在审查第三季度的基础设施扩展计划——后备路线就在变更记录中。

sentences.en: And Alex's screen was now running a validation script that happened to read that route's output.
sentences.zh: Alex 的屏幕现在正在运行一个验证脚本，该脚本碰巧读取了该路由的输出。

blocks.html_en: "That fallback."

sentences.en: "That fallback."
sentences.zh: “那个后备力量。”

blocks.html_en: Alex's fingers paused over the keyboard.

sentences.en: Alex's fingers paused over the keyboard.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯的手指停在键盘上。

blocks.html_en: Mike didn't wait for an answer. He kept walking.

sentences.en: Mike didn't wait for an answer.
sentences.zh: 迈克没有等待答案。

sentences.en: He kept walking.
sentences.zh: 他继续走着。

blocks.html_en: The Bite

sentences.en: The Bite
sentences.zh: 咬伤

blocks.html_en: Day seven. 2:14 AM. The watermark detection script triggered.

sentences.en: Day seven.
sentences.zh: 第七天。

sentences.en: The watermark detection script triggered.
sentences.zh: 触发水印检测脚本。

blocks.html_en: Three tagged training records had appeared in an external S3 bucket:

sentences.en: Three tagged training records had appeared in an external S3 bucket:
sentences.zh: 外部 S3 存储桶中出现了三个标记的训练记录：

blocks.html_en: s3://acl-data-lake-apse1/inbound/medtech/training/

sentences.en: s3://acl-data-lake-apse1/inbound/medtech/training/

blocks.html_en: Alex traced the data flow backward. The path from the shadow pipeline was clean: CI/CD runner → a microservice called data-validate-svc → external S3.

sentences.en: Alex traced the data flow backward.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯向后追踪数据流。

sentences.en: The path from the shadow pipeline was clean: CI/CD runner → a microservice called data-validate-svc → external S3.
sentences.zh: 来自影子管道的路径很干净：CI/CD 运行程序 → 名为 data-validate-svc 的微服务 → 外部 S3。

blocks.html_en: He checked the deployment history of that service. data-validate-svc had been running in MedTech's K8s cluster for five months — deployed on a Friday night, from the account of an SRE who no longer worked there. Permissions never revoked.

sentences.en: He checked the deployment history of that service.
sentences.zh: 他检查了该服务的部署历史记录。

sentences.en: data-validate-svc had been running in MedTech's K8s cluster for five months — deployed on a Friday night, from the account of an SRE who no longer worked there.
sentences.zh: data-validate-svc 已经在 MedTech 的 K8s 集群中运行了五个月——在周五晚上部署，来自不再在那里工作的 SRE 的帐户。

sentences.en: Permissions never revoked.
sentences.zh: 权限从未被撤销。

blocks.html_en: He followed the service's logs. No human was operating it.

sentences.en: He followed the service's logs.
sentences.zh: 他跟踪了该服务的日志。

sentences.en: No human was operating it.
sentences.zh: 没有人操作它。

blocks.html_en: Between 3 AM and 4 AM, it completed the following tasks: received shadow pipeline output → validated data format → dumped to external S3 → cleaned local cache. No gaps, no fluctuation, no trace of manual intervention.

sentences.en: Between 3 AM and 4 AM, it completed the following tasks: received shadow pipeline output → validated data format → dumped to external S3 → cleaned local cache.
sentences.zh: 凌晨 3 点到 4 点之间，它完成了以下任务：接收影子管道输出 → 验证数据格式 → 转储到外部 S3 → 清理本地缓存。

sentences.en: No gaps, no fluctuation, no trace of manual intervention.
sentences.zh: 没有间隙，没有波动，没有人工干预的痕迹。

blocks.html_en: Not someone operating remotely. An unattended extraction pipeline, deployed five months ago, running silent.

sentences.en: Not someone operating remotely.
sentences.zh: 不是远程操作的人。

sentences.en: An unattended extraction pipeline, deployed five months ago, running silent.
sentences.zh: 五个月前部署的一条无人值守的提取管道，静静地运行着。

blocks.html_en: Alex stared at the deployment date on his screen. Five months. Nearly a hundred and fifty days of training data, all passing through its hands.

sentences.en: Alex stared at the deployment date on his screen.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯盯着屏幕上的部署日期。

sentences.en: Five months.
sentences.zh: 五个月。

sentences.en: Nearly a hundred and fifty days of training data, all passing through its hands.
sentences.zh: 近一百五十天的训练数据，全部经过它的手。

blocks.html_en: Alex didn't act immediately. He waited three days to confirm the pipeline's sync cycle — every 72 hours, on schedule, no misses.

sentences.en: Alex didn't act immediately.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯没有立即行动。

sentences.en: He waited three days to confirm the pipeline's sync cycle — every 72 hours, on schedule, no misses.
sentences.zh: 他等了三天才确认管道的同步周期——每 72 小时一次，按计划进行，没有遗漏。

blocks.html_en: Then he walked into Mike's office and closed the door.

sentences.en: Then he walked into Mike's office and closed the door.
sentences.zh: 然后他走进迈克的办公室，关上了门。

blocks.html_en: He opened his notebook. Page corner marked with two numbers: 1.5% and 2:14AM.

sentences.en: He opened his notebook.
sentences.zh: 他打开笔记本。

sentences.en: Page corner marked with two numbers: 1.5% and 2:14AM.
sentences.zh: 页角标有两个数字：1.5% 和 2:14AM。

blocks.html_en: "The training pipeline has a backdoor. Sterilization compliance data has been siphoned at 1.5% per week for five months. The source is ACL."

sentences.en: "The training pipeline has a backdoor.
sentences.zh: “培训渠道有后门。

sentences.en: Sterilization compliance data has been siphoned at 1.5% per week for five months.
sentences.zh: 五个月来，灭菌合规性数据以每周 1.5% 的速度被抽取。

sentences.en: The source is ACL."
sentences.zh: 来源是 ACL。”

blocks.html_en: Four sentences. No conclusion.

sentences.en: Four sentences.
sentences.zh: 四句话。

sentences.en: No conclusion.
sentences.zh: 没有结论。

blocks.html_en: Mike leaned back.

sentences.en: Mike leaned back.
sentences.zh: 迈克向后靠了靠。

blocks.html_en: "Go on."
That evening, hallway. Alex and Mike passed each other. Mike said:

sentences.en: That evening, hallway.
sentences.zh: 那天晚上，走廊。

sentences.en: Alex and Mike passed each other.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯和迈克擦身而过。

sentences.en: Mike said:
sentences.zh: 迈克说：

blocks.html_en: "Is that exit still active?"

sentences.en: "Is that exit still active?"
sentences.zh: “那个出口还有效吗？”

blocks.html_en: "It is."
Mike nodded. Didn't stop walking.

sentences.en: Mike nodded.
sentences.zh: 迈克点点头。

sentences.en: Didn't stop walking.
sentences.zh: 没有停下脚步。

blocks.html_en: The Noise

sentences.en: The Noise
sentences.zh: 噪音

blocks.html_en: Alex didn't pull the extraction pipeline.

sentences.en: Alex didn't pull the extraction pipeline.
sentences.zh: 亚历克斯没有拉动提取管道。

blocks.html_en: He added a new logic layer to the shadow pipeline: wrap the watermarked data in a fake encryption shell, disguised as MedTech's internal archive format for a retired project. The extraction pipeline would sync it to ACL's S3 bucket as before — but ACL would read nothing but unparseable archive noise.

sentences.en: He added a new logic layer to the shadow pipeline: wrap the watermarked data in a fake encryption shell, disguised as MedTech's internal archive format for a retired project.
sentences.zh: 他在影子管道中添加了一个新的逻辑层：将带水印的数据包装在一个虚假的加密外壳中，伪装成 MedTech 一个退役项目的内部存档格式。

sentences.en: The extraction pipeline would sync it to ACL's S3 bucket as before — but ACL would read nothing but unparseable archive noise.
sentences.zh: 提取管道将像以前一样将其同步到 ACL 的 S3 存储桶，但 ACL 除了无法解析的存档噪音之外什么都不会读取。

blocks.html_en: He knew it was a double-edged sword — if the other side monitored data quality, a sudden shift to noise was itself a disclosure signal. But he bet that a pipeline that had run error-free for five months wouldn't have its output reviewed in real time.

sentences.en: He knew it was a double-edged sword — if the other side monitored data quality, a sudden shift to noise was itself a disclosure signal.
sentences.zh: 他知道这是一把双刃剑——如果对方监控数据质量，突然转向噪音本身就是一个泄露信号。

sentences.en: But he bet that a pipeline that had run error-free for five months wouldn't have its output reviewed in real time.
sentences.zh: 但他打赌，已经无错误运行了五个月的管道将不会对其输出进行实时审查。

blocks.html_en: He wrote a third number in his notebook: data-validate-svc.

sentences.en: He wrote a third number in his notebook: data-validate-svc.
sentences.zh: 他在笔记本上写下了第三个数字：data-validate-svc。

blocks.html_en: The tool is still yours. The route is now mine.

sentences.en: The tool is still yours.
sentences.zh: 该工具仍然是您的。

sentences.en: The route is now mine.
sentences.zh: 路线现在是我的了。

blocks.html_en: The Key

sentences.en: The Key
sentences.zh: 关键

blocks.html_en: Tracing the extraction pipeline's full data flow, Alex noticed a detail: data passed through an intermediate service that wasn't on any architecture diagram. data-timeline-aligner. Unremarkable name. K8s labels: team: infra-optimization. Deployed three months ago.

sentences.en: Tracing the extraction pipeline's full data flow, Alex noticed a detail: data passed through an intermediate service that wasn't on any architecture diagram.
sentences.zh: 在跟踪提取管道的完整数据流时，Alex 注意到一个细节：数据通过不在任何架构图上的中间服务传递。

sentences.en: data-timeline-aligner.
sentences.zh: 数据时间线对齐器。

sentences.en: Unremarkable name.
sentences.zh: 不起眼的名字。

sentences.en: K8s labels: team: infra-optimization.
sentences.zh: K8s 标签：团队：基础设施优化。

sentences.en: Deployed three months ago.
sentences.zh: 三个月前部署。

blocks.html_en: He looked at its input and output. It did timestamp alignment — but it was aligning a timestamp format that didn't belong to any MedTech data source.

sentences.en: He looked at its input and output.
sentences.zh: 他查看了它的输入和输出。

sentences.en: It did timestamp alignment — but it was aligning a timestamp format that didn't belong to any MedTech data source.
sentences.zh: 它确实进行了时间戳对齐 - 但它正在对齐不属于任何 MedTech 数据源的时间戳格式。

blocks.html_en: He read the source.

sentences.en: He read the source.
sentences.zh: 他读了来源。

blocks.html_en: Short. A standard Python ETL script — read S3, convert timestamps, write back to S3. No hidden network calls, no suspicious external dependencies.

sentences.en: Short.
sentences.zh: 短的。

sentences.en: A standard Python ETL script — read S3, convert timestamps, write back to S3.
sentences.zh: 标准 Python ETL 脚本 — 读取 S3、转换时间戳、写回 S3。

sentences.en: No hidden network calls, no suspicious external dependencies.
sentences.zh: 没有隐藏的网络调用，没有可疑的外部依赖项。

blocks.html_en: But reading it the third time, he slowed down.

sentences.en: But reading it the third time, he slowed down.
sentences.zh: 但读到第三遍时，他放慢了速度。

blocks.html_en: Not because of what the code did — but how it was written.

sentences.en: Not because of what the code did — but how it was written.
sentences.zh: 不是因为代码做了什么，而是因为它是如何编写的。

blocks.html_en: Every line was a pure function. No classes, no decorators. Variable names short enough to be unambiguous: ts_src, dst_fmt, buf_size — everything that didn't carry information had been cut.

sentences.en: Every line was a pure function.
sentences.zh: 每行都是一个纯函数。

sentences.en: No classes, no decorators.
sentences.zh: 没有类，没有装饰器。

sentences.en: Variable names short enough to be unambiguous: ts_src, dst_fmt, buf_size — everything that didn't carry information had been cut.
sentences.zh: 变量名称足够短，不会产生歧义：ts_src、dst_fmt、buf_size — 所有不携带信息的内容都已被删除。

blocks.html_en: He compared it against other services in ACL's pipeline. ACL's code didn't look like this — every line had team tags and compliance comments. Tidy. Traceable. No personality. This one was different.

sentences.en: He compared it against other services in ACL's pipeline.
sentences.zh: 他将其与 ACL 管道中的其他服务进行了比较。

sentences.en: ACL's code didn't look like this — every line had team tags and compliance comments.
sentences.zh: ACL 的代码看起来并不像这样 — 每行都有团队标签和合规性注释。

sentences.en: Tidy.
sentences.zh: 整齐的。

sentences.en: Traceable.
sentences.zh: 可追溯。

sentences.en: No personality.
sentences.zh: 没有个性。

sentences.en: This one was different.
sentences.zh: 这个不一样。

blocks.html_en: He checked the CPU curve again. This service's data throughput didn't match its claimed timestamp-alignment workload — it was processing three times the data a simple alignment task would need. It wasn't just aligning timestamps. It was doing something else.

sentences.en: He checked the CPU curve again.
sentences.zh: 他再次检查了CPU曲线。

sentences.en: This service's data throughput didn't match its claimed timestamp-alignment workload — it was processing three times the data a simple alignment task would need.
sentences.zh: 该服务的数据吞吐量与其声称的时间戳对齐工作负载不匹配——它处理的数据是简单对齐任务所需数据的三倍。

sentences.en: It wasn't just aligning timestamps.
sentences.zh: 这不仅仅是对齐时间戳。

sentences.en: It was doing something else.
sentences.zh: 它正在做别的事情。

blocks.html_en: He paused. He didn't look at just this one service. He wrote a local script — pull every Pod with a Python container from the K8s namespace the extraction pipeline lived in, fingerprint the source code of each.

sentences.en: He paused.
sentences.zh: 他停了下来。

sentences.en: He didn't look at just this one service.
sentences.zh: 他不只看重这一项服务。

sentences.en: He wrote a local script — pull every Pod with a Python container from the K8s namespace the extraction pipeline lived in, fingerprint the source code of each.
sentences.zh: 他编写了一个本地脚本——从提取管道所在的 K8s 命名空间中提取带有 Python 容器的每个 Pod，对每个 Pod 的源代码进行指纹识别。

sentences.zh: 输出将其自身排列成表格：

blocks.html_en: Same

Four services. Same hand. Spread across pipeline, storage, and monitoring — three different layers. Each one doing something that looked normal but shouldn't exist. Together, they formed an observation net that ACL itself didn't know about.

sentences.en: Four services.
sentences.zh: 四项服务。

sentences.en: Same hand.
sentences.zh: 同一只手。

sentences.en: Spread across pipeline, storage, and monitoring — three different layers.
sentences.zh: 分布在管道、存储和监控三个不同的层。

sentences.en: Each one doing something that looked normal but shouldn't exist.
sentences.zh: 每个人都在做一些看似正常但不应该存在的事情。

sentences.en: Together, they formed an observation net that ACL itself didn't know about.

content_html_zh:
扔出一块砖头来引诱翡翠宝石。 ——三十六计，抛砖引玉

本系列之前的内容：

#8：亚历克斯目睹了人工智能仪表板的接管。 — MedTech 签署了一份价值百万美元的人工智能监控合同。 亚历克斯在训练期间建立了一个隐藏面板。 当面板出现故障时，只有他可以读写生产。

#15：Derek 和 Alex 共享一台服务器。 ACL 的 AI 正在倾听两者的声音。 — Alex 和 Derek 伪造警报以转移 ACL 的审计工程师的注意力，然后重定向收集器输出。 Alex 永远不会忘记 ACL 的管道签名。

亚历克斯没有看警报。 他观察模式。

警报会告诉您发生了什么问题。 模式告诉您正在发生什么变化。

星期一早上，凌晨 2 点。 他拉出了 MedTech 的 AI 监测平台过去十六周的灵敏度曲线。 三行：异常检测率、误报率、覆盖率。 前两者在正常范围内波动。 第三个——灭菌合规性异常检测——每周下降 1.5%。

曲线太平滑了。 不是随机噪声。

他检查了模型漂移、数据转移、配置更改。 如果是自然漂移，曲线就不是线性的——漂移有加速阶段和平稳阶段。 它不会每周精确地下降 1.5%。 有人正在按时间表制定指标。

他找不到污染源。 但他可以追踪受污染数据的去向。

诱饵

Alex 花了四天时间在 CI/CD 管道内构建影子训练管道。

它与 MedTech 的真实训练流程相同——相同的 API 端点、数据格式、身份验证协议、日志格式。 无法区分。

一个区别是：流经此管道的数据将获得一个不可见的签名 - 嵌入在每个训练样本的浮点特征值的最低有效位中的 128 位哈希，由样本 ID 和管道密钥生成。 Float32 尾数有 23 位。 修改 LSB 会引入 10⁻⁷ 级的误差——对模型训练精度的影响可以忽略不计。

他在 CI/CD 路由配置中的 train-pipeline-fallback 下注册了影子管道地址，权重为 12%。 旁边有一条评论：

# fallback pipeline active — 12% load distribution per Q3 infra scaling plan

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

看起来完全正常。 灰度流量分割。

如果有人查看 CI/CD 配置，他们会阅读例行回退路由。 Alex 只是在等待，看看来自 12% 端的数据是否会流向不该流向的地方。

午饭后，迈克走过亚历克斯的办公桌，停了两秒钟。 那天早上他一直在审查第三季度的基础设施扩展计划——后备路线就在变更记录中。 Alex 的屏幕现在正在运行一个验证脚本，该脚本碰巧读取了该路由的输出。

“那个后备力量。”

亚历克斯的手指停在键盘上。

迈克没有等待答案。 他继续走着。

咬伤

第七天。 凌晨 2:14。 触发水印检测脚本。

外部 S3 存储桶中出现了三个标记的训练记录：

s3://acl-data-lake-apse1/inbound/medtech/training/

亚历克斯向后追踪数据流。 来自影子管道的路径很干净：CI/CD 运行程序 → 名为 data-validate-svc 的微服务 → 外部 S3。

他检查了该服务的部署历史记录。 data-validate-svc 已经在 MedTech 的 K8s 集群中运行了五个月——在周五晚上部署，来自不再在那里工作的 SRE 的帐户。 权限从未被撤销。

他跟踪了该服务的日志。 没有人操作它。

凌晨 3 点到 4 点之间，它完成了以下任务：接收影子管道输出 → 验证数据格式 → 转储到外部 S3 → 清理本地缓存。 没有间隙，没有波动，没有人工干预的痕迹。

不是远程操作的人。 五个月前部署的一条无人值守的提取管道，静静地运行着。

亚历克斯盯着屏幕上的部署日期。 五个月。 近一百五十天的训练数据，全部经过它的手。

亚历克斯没有立即行动。 他等了三天才确认管道的同步周期——每 72 小时一次，按计划进行，没有遗漏。

然后他走进迈克的办公室，关上了门。

他打开笔记本。 页角标有两个数字：1.5% 和 2:14AM。

“培训渠道有后门。 五个月来，灭菌合规性数据以每周 1.5% 的速度被抽取。 来源是 ACL。”

四句话。 没有结论。

迈克向后靠了靠。

"Go on."

那天晚上，走廊。 亚历克斯和迈克擦身而过。 迈克说：

“那个出口还有效吗？”

"It is."

迈克点点头。 没有停下脚步。

噪音

亚历克斯没有拉动提取管道。

他在影子管道中添加了一个新的逻辑层：将带水印的数据包装在一个虚假的加密外壳中，伪装成 MedTech 一个退役项目的内部存档格式。 提取管道将像以前一样将其同步到 ACL 的 S3 存储桶，但 ACL 除了无法解析的存档噪音之外什么都不会读取。

他知道这是一把双刃剑——如果对方监控数据质量，突然转向噪音本身就是一个泄露信号。 但他打赌，已经无错误运行了五个月的管道将不会对其输出进行实时审查。

他在笔记本上写下了第三个数字：data-validate-svc。

该工具仍然是您的。 路线现在是我的了。

关键

在跟踪提取管道的完整数据流时，Alex 注意到一个细节：数据通过不在任何架构图上的中间服务传递。 数据时间线对齐器。 不起眼的名字。 K8s 标签：团队：基础设施优化。 三个月前部署。

他查看了它的输入和输出。 它确实进行了时间戳对齐 - 但它正在对齐不属于任何 MedTech 数据源的时间戳格式。

他读了来源。

短的。 标准 Python ETL 脚本 — 读取 S3、转换时间戳、写回 S3。 没有隐藏的网络调用，没有可疑的外部依赖项。

但读到第三遍时，他放慢了速度。
